company_type: Information Management Services and Data Center Operations
segments: Global Records and Information Management (Global RIM) Business

dependencies: Third party telecommunications carriers for internet connectivity to data centers
Third parties to provide power to data centers
Design firms, general contractors, and subcontractors for data center construction
Suppliers of electricity and water for cooling at data centers
risks: Supply chain and logistical challenges affecting construction costs
Disruptions to supply chain could affect cost or timing of expansion projects
Constrained supplies of new power for data center expansion
Third party telecommunications carriers may elect not to offer services or discontinue services
Third party power providers may fail to deliver power in adequate quantities or on a consistent basis
Rising construction costs reflecting increase in cost of labor and raw materials

regulatory_risks: Data privacy and cybersecurity laws and regulations that are complex, change frequently, and have become more stringent over time
Data breach notification requirements and increased enforcement of regulations regarding use, access, accuracy and security of personal information
Emerging AI regulations and integration of AI tools creating additional compliance and cybersecurity risks
Environmental laws and regulations that could increase compliance costs or impose limitations on operations
Regulations and permit requirements governing air pollutants from emergency generators at data centers
Regulations on ozone-depleting substances in HVAC and fire suppression systems
Fire and safety standards compliance costs
Environmental standards for contaminated real estate (hazardous substances, underground storage tanks, asbestos)
Future regulatory action and environmental laws may impose costs for environmental compliance that do not exist today
Sanctions and anti-corruption laws such as Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act
Trade policies or environmental regulations that restrict or increase cost of exporting IT assets (particularly to China)
Regulatory requirements under United States Government contracts
OECD Pillar Two global minimum tax rate of 15% implemented in many foreign jurisdictions
Changes in tax laws, regulations and administrative interpretations that may have retroactive application
State income tax regimes that do not follow federal REIT rules

sanction_risks: Sanctions compliance requirements under Foreign Corrupt Practices Act and UK Bribery Act
Trade policies that restrict exporting IT assets to China or other markets
Geopolitical events such as war or other military conflict creating additional risk in global operations
